parameters.content:
## 🎬 Short-Form Video Automation MVP

### Full Flow
1. **Fetch TikTok Trends** — Apify scrapes trend metadata (no videos downloaded)
2. **Engagement Scoring** — Ranks trends by weighted score
3. **AI Video Idea** — GPT-4o creates an *original* concept (not a copy)
4. **Creatomate Render** — Generates a 9:16 vertical video from a template
5. **Telegram Approval** — Sends preview + approve/reject links
6. **Upload-Post Publish** — Posts approved video to TikTok
7. **Google Sheets Log** — Records every run for tracking

### Quick Start
1. Set all env vars in **n8n Settings → Environment Variables**
2. Run the **Manual Trigger** to test end-to-end
3. Enable **Schedule Trigger** for daily automation

See `README.md` and `.env.example` for full setup instructions.

## 🔑 Required Environment Variables

Set these in **n8n Settings → Environment Variables**:

```
APIFY_API_TOKEN
APIFY_ACTOR_ID
OPENAI_API_KEY
CREATOMATE_API_KEY
CREATOMATE_TEMPLATE_ID
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID
UPLOAD_POST_API_KEY
UPLOAD_POST_ACCOUNT_ID
GOOGLE_SHEET_ID
GOOGLE_SHEETS_ACCESS_TOKEN
```

See `.env.example` for descriptions.
For Google Sheets token, use a service account or OAuth2 bearer token.

## ⚠️ Original Content Policy

✅ Fetches trend **metadata only** (caption, hashtags, counts)
✅ Uses trends as **creative inspiration** — not as source material
✅ AI prompt explicitly says: *"Do not copy the original video"*
✅ Creatomate renders a **new original video** from your template

❌ Does NOT download original TikTok videos
❌ Does NOT reupload or repost any creator's content
❌ Does NOT claim ownership of trending topics

Always review AI output before publishing.

## 🎬 Creatomate Template Requirements

Your template must use these **element names**:

| Element Name | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `video_title` | Text | Main title overlay |
| `scene_1_text` | Text | Scene 1 description |
| `scene_2_text` | Text | Scene 2 description |
| `scene_3_text` | Text | Scene 3 description |
| `on_screen_text_1` | Text | Caption line 1 |
| `on_screen_text_2` | Text | Caption line 2 |
| `product_category` | Text | Category label |
| `product_name` | Text | Top product |
| `cta_text` | Text | Call to action |
| `voiceover` | Audio | AI-generated script |

**Format:** Vertical 9:16 (1080×1920)
**Duration:** 15–60 seconds

## 📱 Telegram Approval Setup

### Create your bot
1. Message **@BotFather** → `/newbot`
2. Copy the token → `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`
3. Add bot to your group or use direct chat
4. Get chat ID → `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID`

### How approval works
- Bot sends video URL + full details
- Two clickable links are included:
  - ✅ **Approve** → calls resume webhook with `approved=true`
  - ❌ **Reject** → calls resume webhook with `approved=false`
- Workflow resumes automatically when you click either link

### Note
`$execution.resumeUrl` is generated by the **Wait for Approval** node.

nodes.name: Manual Trigger
nodes.notes: Click 'Test workflow' to run once manually. Use this during setup and testing before enabling the schedule.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger (Daily 9AM)
nodes.notes: Runs daily at 09:00 server time. Activate the workflow to enable this trigger. Adjust cron expression as needed (e.g. '0 8 * * 1-5' for weekdays at 8AM).

nodes.name: Fetch TikTok Trends (Apify)
nodes.notes:
Fetches trending TikTok METADATA only via Apify scraper — no videos are downloaded.

Recommended Actor: 'clockworks~tiktok-hashtag-search' or 'clockworks~free-tiktok-scraper'
Set APIFY_ACTOR_ID to the actor's ID (e.g. 'clockworks~tiktok-hashtag-search').

The sync endpoint blocks until results are ready (up to 120s).

nodes.name: Calculate Engagement Score
nodes.notes:
Scores every fetched trend and returns the single top performer.
Formula: ((likes×1) + (comments×3) + (shares×5)) / views

nodes.name: Build OpenAI Request
nodes.notes:
Builds the full OpenAI API request body.
The prompt explicitly instructs the AI to create original content — not to copy the trend video.
Outputs the request object so the HTTP node can send it directly.

nodes.name: AI Creates Video Idea (OpenAI)
nodes.notes:
Sends the prepared request to GPT-4o.
The model is instructed to return structured JSON only.
Uses OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable.

nodes.name: Parse AI Response
nodes.notes:
Parses and validates the OpenAI JSON response.
Throws a descriptive error if the response is malformed or missing required fields.
Ensures all array fields are actually arrays before downstream use.

nodes.name: Build Creatomate Request
nodes.notes:
Maps AI video concept fields to Creatomate template element names.
EDIT THIS NODE if your Creatomate template uses different element names.
The modifications keys must exactly match the element names in your template.

nodes.name: Generate Video (Creatomate)
nodes.notes:
Submits render job to Creatomate.
Returns immediately with a render ID and status 'planned' or 'rendering'.
The workflow then waits 60s before polling for completion.
Uses CREATOMATE_API_KEY env var.

nodes.name: Extract Render ID
nodes.notes:
Extracts the render_id from Creatomate's response.
This ID is used to poll the render status after waiting.

nodes.name: Wait 60s for Render
nodes.notes:
Waits 60 seconds before polling Creatomate for render completion.
Increase this value for longer or more complex templates.
For very long renders, consider implementing a retry loop.

nodes.name: Check Render Status
nodes.notes:
Polls Creatomate for the render result.
Expected status values: 'planned', 'rendering', 'succeeded', 'failed'.
For MVP: single poll after 60s. Add a loop for more robust retry logic.

nodes.name: Is Render Complete?
nodes.notes:
TRUE (output 1): status = 'succeeded' → send to Telegram for approval
FALSE (output 2): render failed or still pending → stop and log

nodes.name: Build Telegram Message
nodes.notes:
Builds the approval message with all video details and approve/reject links.
$execution.resumeUrl is the unique URL generated by the Wait for Approval node.
Visiting this URL with ?approved=true or ?approved=false resumes the workflow.

nodes.name: Send Video to Telegram
nodes.notes:
Sends the approval message to your Telegram bot chat.
Uses TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID env vars.
The message includes clickable Approve/Reject links.

nodes.name: Wait for Approval (Webhook)
nodes.notes:
Pauses the workflow until the approve or reject URL is visited.
The resume URL was included in the Telegram message.

Query parameters passed in the URL become available as $json.query
after this node resumes (e.g. $json.query.approved).

No timeout is set — the execution waits indefinitely. Set a timeout in
production to auto-reject stale approvals.

nodes.name: Check Approval
nodes.notes:
TRUE (output 1): approved=true → publish the video
FALSE (output 2): approved=false or no value → log rejection and stop

nodes.name: Build Upload-Post Request
nodes.notes:
Builds the publish request for Upload-Post.
Currently set to TikTok only. Add 'instagram', 'youtube' etc. to the platforms array when ready.
The full caption includes the AI-generated caption and hashtags.

nodes.name: Publish with Upload-Post
nodes.notes:
Posts the video to TikTok via Upload-Post.
Uses UPLOAD_POST_API_KEY and UPLOAD_POST_ACCOUNT_ID env vars.

NOTE: Verify the exact Upload-Post API endpoint and payload format
in their documentation — the endpoint URL may differ for your plan.
Instagram/Facebook publishing can be added by extending the platforms array.

nodes.name: Build Sheets Log Row
nodes.notes:
Prepares the log row. Pulls data from multiple upstream nodes.
Column order must match the header row in your Google Sheet.
See README for how to set up the sheet and access token.

nodes.name: Log to Google Sheets
nodes.notes:
Appends a row to your Google Sheet log via the Sheets API.
Requires GOOGLE_SHEET_ID and a valid GOOGLE_SHEETS_ACCESS_TOKEN.

Alternative: Replace this node with the native 'Google Sheets' node
and use n8n's built-in Google OAuth2 credential for easier token management.

Sheet columns (A→K): date | trend_url | engagement_score | video_url |
caption | product_category | approval | publish_status | publish_url |
video_title | products

nodes.name: Log Rejection
nodes.notes:
Records the rejection reason and stops this execution branch.
Extend this node to log the rejection to Google Sheets or send a follow-up Telegram notification.

nodes.name: Stop - Render Failed
nodes.notes:
Handles Creatomate render failure.
Logs the error and stops this execution branch.
Consider extending with a Telegram notification for render failures in production.
